zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere於1306年1月25日出生在英國賀福爾布拉可麥時，他的父親Fulk Strange40歲，母親Eleanor Giffard32歲。他於1327年與Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1346年登記服兵役。他於1330年被列為member of parliament。他去世於1349年7月21日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere于1306年1月25日出生在英国賀福爾布拉可麥时，他的父亲Fulk Strange40岁，母亲Eleanor Giffard32岁。他于1327年与Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1346年登记服兵役。他于1330年被列为member of parliament。他去世于1349年7月21日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere was born on 25 January 1306, in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, his father, Fulk Strange, was 40 and his mother, Eleanor Giffard, was 32. He married Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer in 1327. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He registered for military service in 1346. In 1330, his occupation is listed as member of parliament. He died on 21 July 1349, in Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere est né le 25 janvier 1306, à Blakemere, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Fulk Strange, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Giffard, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer en 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1346. En 1330, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 1349, à Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere am 25. Januar 1306 in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fulk Strange 40 und seine Mutter Eleanor Giffard 32. Er hat 1327 Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er hat sich 1346 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1330 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament aufgeführt. Er starb am 21. Juli 1349 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere è nato il il 25 gennaio 1306 nel Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Fulk Strange, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Giffard, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer nel 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1346. Nel 1330, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament. Morì il 21 luglio 1349, nel Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmereが1306年1月25日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブラケメアで生まれた時，父Fulk Strangeは40歳，母Eleanor Giffardは32歳であった。1327年にLady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1346年に兵役登録をした。1330年，職業はmember of parliamentと記録されている。彼は1349年7月21日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアセジェブルークで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere(이)가 1306년 1월 25일 영국 허퍼드실르 블레이케머에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fulk Strange(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Giffard(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1327년 Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1346년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1330년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament이었다. 그는 1349년 7월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 세지브룩에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.25 January 1306–21 July 1349: 25 de janeiro de 1306–21 de julho de 1349
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere nasceu em 25 de janeiro de 1306, em Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Fulk Strange, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Giffard, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer em 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1346. Em 1330, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament. Ele faleceu em 21 de julho de 1349, em Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere родился 25 января 1306 г. в местности блеикемир, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Fulk Strange было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Giffard, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer в 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1346 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1330 годуработал по профессии member of parliament. Он умер 21 июля 1349 г. в местности седжебрук, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет.

sm.between 1330 and 1343: mai le 1330 e o'o I le 1343

es.25 January 1306–21 July 1349: 25 de enero de 1306–21 de julio de 1349
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere nació el 25 de enero de 1306, en Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Fulk Strange, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Eleanor Giffard, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer en 1327. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1346. En 1330, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament. Murió el 21 de julio de 1349, en Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años.
